channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US judge tosses out Musk’s bid to void Twitter fraud verdict
item.description: Jurors had found owner Elon Musk liable for tweets questioning whether Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts.

item.title: Crowds hold ‘destroy Israel’ banner at Spain’s Pamplona bull run
item.description: A giant banner reading ‘Destroy Israel’ was unfurled as thousands gathered for the San Fermin bull run festival.

item.title: Prosecutors in US lay out case against alleged killer of Charlie Kirk
item.description: Judge to decide whether there's enough evidence to bring Tyler Robinson to trial for conservative activist's killing.

item.title: What is the fallout of the increasing violence in Mali?
item.description: Coalition of rebels and armed groups carries out another series of attacks.

item.title: US Supreme Court clears path for Texas to enforce app age verification law
item.description: Parents must approve app downloads for minors as legal fight over Texas age verification law continues.

item.title: Body of Iran’s slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei arrives in Qom
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s body arrives in Qom as millions of mourners honour his leadership over past three days.

item.title: NATO urges members to deliver plans for reaching defence spending targets
item.description: US warns of consequences for NATO members without clear spending plans, signalling high expectations from allies.

item.title: Pro-Palestine protesters rally for ‘Filton 25’ activists at UK court
item.description: Pro-Palestine protesters rally for 'Filton 25’ activists at UK court.

item.title: NATO chief calls for ‘credible plans’ to reach defence spending goals
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said members are now spending around 4% of GDP on defence.

item.title: Nigeria sees no sign that anti-immigrant violence is waning in South Africa
item.description: South Africa's government has been accused of not doing enough to tackle xenophobic attacks.

item.title: Left-wing candidate Roberto Sanchez concedes Peru vote to Keiko Fujimori
item.description: Announcement comes days after Peru's electoral agency certified right-wing Fujimori as winner in razor-thin race.

item.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 500
item.description: Nation’s 17th Ebola epidemic is fuelled by the Bundibugyo virus, with no existing vaccine or approved treatment options.

item.title: Trump rings in new ‘Trump Account’ investment accounts for children
item.description: US President Donald Trump rang the opening bells of the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq to welcome 'Trump Accounts'.

item.title: Iran reappoints a familiar face to powerful post during Khamenei funeral
item.description: The judiciary chief has emphasised the need to avenge Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and prosecute foreign 'aggressors'.

item.title: Flavio Bolsonaro asks Trump to delay tariffs on Brazil until after election
item.description: President Lula accuses Jair Bolsonaro's son, now a presidential hopeful, of helping trigger proposed US tariffs.

item.title: Ticket prices continue to fall for USA-Belgium World Cup last-16 clash
item.description: Get-in prices for the round of 16 football game at Seattle Stadium have fallen by 27 percent over the past three days.

item.title: Belgian football body challenges reversal of Balogun’s World Cup suspension
item.description: Trump says he asked FIFA's president to review USA player Balogun's game suspension, but did not tell him what to do.

item.title: What makes this NATO summit high stakes?
item.description: Al Jazeera’s James Bays explains why NATO’s Ankara summit is high stakes.

item.title: Balogun, Garrincha and Pinochet: FIFA’s controversial World Cup decisions
item.description: The Balogun affair has shocked football, but FIFA's showpiece has a long history of fixes, farces and failures.

item.title: Khamenei family mourns, but Mojtaba’s absence fuels public insecurity
item.description: Many Tehran residents say the absence of Iran's new supreme leader makes them question their own security.

item.title: UN rights body demands release of Palestinian doctor detained by Israel
item.description: Hussam Abu Safia's family and lawyer say his life is in imminent danger and shows signs of having been tortured.

item.title: Ex-Syrian intelligence chief in Raqqa convicted of torture in Austria
item.description: Khaled al-Halabi denied involvement, but the court deemed him responsible for abuse of detainees in Raqqa.

item.title: Argentina vs Egypt: FIFA World Cup last 16 – Messi, Salah, prediction, news
item.description: Lionel Messi's Argentina go up against Mohamed Salah's Egypt in a spicy World Cup round of 16 encounter.

item.title: Lebanon latest: Teacher killed by Israeli airstrike
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Heidi Pett brings you the latest from Lebanon, where Israeli strikes continue despite a ‘ceasefire’.

item.title: Portugal vs Spain LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 round of 16
item.description: Live coverage and text updates of the World Cup last-16 clash between Portugal and Spain in Dallas in the US.
